items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project moves forward
items.summary: San José is proceeding with actions related to replacing elevators at the terminal (likely the transit center). This infrastructure upgrade affects how residents and commuters access the facility.

items.headline: City seeks bids for encampment cleanup services
items.summary: The city released a request for proposals from companies to handle encampment cleanup, indicating a formal effort to address homeless encampments across San José through contracted services.

items.headline: Digital advertising deal amended with Clear Channel Outdoor
items.summary: San José modified its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor for large digital billboards on city property, potentially affecting how advertising appears across the city and what revenue the city receives.

items.headline: City negotiating purchase of Finch Avenue property with school district
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations to buy or lease 10480 Finch Avenue from the Cupertino Union School District, with price and payment terms still being worked out. The outcome could affect what gets developed on this property and how the city uses it.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The city council approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, outlining how green spaces and recreational pathways will be developed in this key area. This plan will shape future parks and outdoor amenities for residents in and around the Innovation District over the coming years.

items.headline: Council considers nine new homes plus accessory units on Old Trace Road
items.summary: A developer is proposing to subdivide a vacant 1-acre lot into nine single-family homes plus seven junior accessory dwelling units (JADUs) in the Residential Estate zone. The City Council will hear this quasi-judicial case, which could add 16 residential units to that neighborhood.

items.headline: Supermicro invests $2.5M in equipment at San José facility
items.summary: The major tech hardware manufacturer is upgrading its Rock Avenue location with $2.5 million in new equipment installation. This represents significant continued investment in San José's tech manufacturing sector.

items.headline: Summerhill redevelopment project advances at El Camino Real
items.summary: Palo Alto Greens HOA is removing and replacing trees as part of the larger Summerhill redevelopment at 4345 El Camino Real. This signals progress on a significant housing/mixed-use project that residents have been tracking.
